risk.verdict_note: Some entries are informational runtime observations and calibration notes; they explain context but are not independent malicious proofs.

calibration.note: False-positive resistant calibration: permissions, domains, exported components, and soft static indicators cannot produce Critical 100% without hard malicious evidence.

mitre_attack_mapping.recommended_action: Review why the app starts after boot.
Run in multiple sandboxes and inspect anti-analysis checks.
Review contacted domains/IPs and block suspicious infrastructure.
Rotate exposed keys and verify if secrets are production credentials.

network_analysis.note: Network analysis combines static strings with emulator artifacts. It is triage evidence, not proof of live C2 behavior.

classifiers.name: Permission Risk Classifier
classifiers.description: Scores permission abuse potential and manifest attack surface.

evidence.title: Permission/combo risk groups
detail: Boot Persistence Plus Foreground Service And Network Access
Audio Capture Permission With Background Service And Network Access
Suspicious Domain Indicators With Network Permission

classifiers.recommendation: Review requested permissions against the app purpose. Pay special attention to SMS, overlay, accessibility, install-packages, and exported components.

classifiers.name: Network Risk Classifier
classifiers.description: Scores static and dynamic network indicators, cleartext traffic, suspicious domains, and runtime connectivity.
classifiers.recommendation: Investigate suspicious domains/IPs, block cleartext HTTP where possible, and validate whether contacted endpoints match the legitimate app backend.

classifiers.name: Secrets Classifier
classifiers.description: Detects hardcoded secrets, crypto indicators, and environment/root-check patterns.

classifiers.recommendation: Rotate exposed credentials, review API keys/tokens, and avoid shipping hardcoded secrets. Validate crypto usage and root-check intent.

classifiers.name: WebView Classifier
classifiers.description: Scores WebView exposure, JavaScript/file-access style indicators, and unsafe HTTP loading.
classifiers.recommendation: Review WebView settings, disable unsafe JavaScript bridges/file access where possible, enforce HTTPS, and restrict loaded domains.

classifiers.name: Dynamic Behavior Classifier
classifiers.description: Scores emulator runtime indicators, dynamic coverage, observed behavior, and cleanup state.

classifiers.recommendation: Prioritize analyst review of runtime behavior. Re-run dynamic analysis with longer duration or targeted interaction if coverage is limited.

classifiers.name: MITRE Classifier
classifiers.description: Summarizes mapped MITRE Mobile tactics/techniques and mapping confidence.
classifiers.recommendation: Use the MITRE mapping to plan analyst review, response actions, and report explanation. Focus on high-confidence tactics first.

summary.note: Specialist classifiers are deterministic, evidence-backed views. They complement the final risk score and help analyst triage.

actions.message: APK would be copied to local quarantine.

recommended_actions: Review the list of domain names and ensure they are properly secured and only accessible to authorized users.
Ensure that all communications involving IP addresses and ports are encrypted.
Review AI findings and run dynamic analysis if not already completed.
Preserve job artifacts and evidence lineage.
Use a disposable emulator for dynamic validation before allowing installation.

calibration.note: Generic runtime observations and permission-heavy samples route to medium analysis unless hard strong evidence is present.

limitations: This report is a triage aid, not a final malware/benign verdict. High-impact decisions should include analyst review and, when appropriate, additional sandboxing or reverse engineering.

Static analysis quality depends on available tooling. apktool improves AndroidManifest/resource decoding; jadx improves Java/Kotlin source inspection. If these tools are unavailable or time out, the report falls back to ZIP/aapt-level evidence.
Obfuscated, packed, encrypted, or dynamically downloaded code may hide behavior from static analysis.
Dynamic analysis coverage is limited by emulator runtime, triggered UI paths, network availability, permissions granted, and anti-emulator behavior. Lack of dynamic findings does not prove safety.
AI findings are restricted to collected evidence references. Unsupported model claims are removed, but remaining findings should still be reviewed by a qualified analyst.
safety_notes: Unknown APKs should only be executed inside a disposable emulator or VM.
AI findings are evidence-grounded and should be reviewed by a qualified analyst for enforcement decisions.
